title: California Smashes ZEV Sales Record in Q3
content: Governor Gavin Newsom announced that Californians purchased 124,755 zero-emission vehicles (ZEVs) in the third quarter of 2025, representing 29.1% of new car sales, the highest quarterly sales share ever reported in California. Despite the numerous setbacks to the ZEV industry brought about by the federal administration, Californians made it clear they want to drive ZEVs. The ZEV market is also diversifying in California, with 146 ZEV models available in Q1, up from 105 models in Q1 of last year. Of the 124,755 ZEVs purchased in Q3, 108,685 of those were EVs. This is also a record for California, with EV sales spiking nearly 30% over Q2 2025. Itâ€™s clear Californians are confident in the growing network of EV chargers across the state and are buoyed by the choices available from auto manufacturers. On October 8, 2025, California became the first state in the nation to adopt EV charger reliability and reporting regulations, laying the foundation for charging station reliability across the nation. The regulation will not only improve the reliability of publicly funded fast chargers but also enable accurate reporting on EV chargers operating in California.
author: Analysts Account
